questions.stem: （インタビューで） 私が30年間歌手を続けてこられたのは、ファンの方の支えがあったからです。 ______ ______ ★ ______ 、歌い続けたいと思っています。
opts: 限り
ファンの皆さんが
応援してくれる
いる
questions.expl: Answer (★ Position) Answer 4: いる（★ is 3rd position） Correct Order 3 → 2 → 4 → 1 応援してくれる → ファンの皆さんが → ★ いる → 限り Chunk-by-Chunk Breakdown ③ 応援してくれる — relative clause “who support (me/us)”; modifies the following noun ② ファンの皆さんが — “the fans” as the subject (marked by が) ④ いる — “exist/there are”; predicates the existence of such fans ① 限り — “as long as …”; attaches to the clause to form a conditional limit Reconstructed Sentence （インタビューで） 私が30年間歌手を続けてこられたのは、ファンの方の支えがあったからです。応援してくれるファンの皆さんがいる限り、歌い続けたいと思っています。 → In an interview: “I’ve been able to keep singing for 30 years thanks to the support of my fans. As long as there are fans who support me, I want to keep singing.” Why This Order Works - 応援してくれる modifies ファンの皆さん to specify “fans who support (me)”. - ファンの皆さんがいる forms the existence statement “there are fans.” - 限り attaches to that clause to mean “as long as there are (such) fans.” - This sets the condition for the speaker’s intention to continue singing.
questions.expl_ko: 정답: 4번 (いる, 정답 위치 ★). 배열: ファンの皆さんが → 応援してくれる → ★(いる) → 限り → 歌い続けたい. 「팬 여러분이 응원해 주시는 한, 노래 계속하고 싶다」. 「~限り」 = 「~하는 한」.

questions.stem: S市が18歳以上の______ ______ ★ ______ 運動不足を感じていることがわかった。
opts: 意識調査を行ったところ、
市民を対象に
多くの人が
運動に関する
questions.expl: Answer (★ Position) Answer 1: 意識調査を行ったところ、 Correct Order 2 → 4 → 1 ★ → 3 市民を対象に → 運動に関する → 意識調査を行ったところ、 → 多くの人が Chunk-by-Chunk Breakdown ② 市民を対象に — “targeting (the) citizens” (marks the survey target) ④ 運動に関する — “concerning exercise” (modifies 調査) ① 意識調査を行ったところ、 — “(we) conducted an attitude survey and found that …” ③ 多くの人が — “many people” (subject of the reported result) Reconstructed Sentence S市が18歳以上の市民を対象に運動に関する意識調査を行ったところ、多くの人が運動不足を感じていることがわかった。 → “In City S, after conducting a survey on attitudes toward exercise targeting residents aged 18 and over, it was found that many people feel they don’t get enough exercise.” Why This Order Works Modifiers (市民を対象に／運動に関する) correctly stack before 意識調査, and 〜を行ったところ introduces the discovered result (多くの人が…わかった).
questions.expl_ko: 정답: 1번 (意識調査を行ったところ, 정답 위치 ★). 배열: 18歳以上の → 市民を対象に → 運動に関する → ★(意識調査を行ったところ) → 多くの人が. 「~의식조사를 한 결과 많은 사람이~」.

questions.stem: 子供のころ、母はしつけに厳しくて、私はそれが嫌だった。しかし、母が______ ______ ★ ______今ならわかる。
opts: 親になった
私のことを
思えばこそだったのだと
厳しかったのは、
questions.expl: Answer (★ Position) Answer 3: 思えばこそだったのだと Correct Order 4 → 2 → 3 ★ → 1 厳しかったのは、 → 私のことを → 思えばこそだったのだと → 親になった Chunk-by-Chunk Breakdown ④ 厳しかったのは、 — topicalizes the strictness: “As for (her) being strict,” ② 私のことを — object: “(because she) cared about me” (sets object for 思う) ③ 思えばこそだったのだと — fixed expression “(it was) precisely because she cared/thought of me” ① 親になった — “(now that I have) become a parent” (sets the time/condition, continues with 今なら) Reconstructed Sentence 子供のころ、母はしつけに厳しくて、私はそれが嫌だった。しかし、母が厳しかったのは、私のことを思えばこそだったのだと親になった今ならわかる。 → “When I was a child, my mother was strict about discipline and I hated it. But now that I’m a parent, I understand that she was strict precisely because she cared about me.” Why This Order Works 〜のは topicalizes the fact; 私のことを思えばこそ supplies the motive; だったのだと embeds that realization; 親になった今 sets the time at which the speaker understands it.
questions.expl_ko: 정답: 4번 (厳しかったのは, 정답 위치 ★). 배열: 母が → 親になった → 私のことを → ★(厳しかったのは) → 思えばこそだったのだと. 잠깐, correct=2(0-idx)면 「思えばこそだったのだと」 ★ 위치. 배열 재확인: 親になった → 厳しかったのは → 私のことを → ★(思えばこそだったのだと). 「엄마가 엄격했던 것은 부모가 되어 보니 나를 생각해서였던 것」.

opts: レインコートは
機能性もさることながら
M社から発売された
そのかわいらしいデザインが話題となり
questions.expl: Answer (★ Position) Answer 2: 機能性もさることながら Correct Order 3 → 1 → 2 ★ → 4 M社から発売された → レインコートは → 機能性もさることながら → そのかわいらしいデザインが話題となり Chunk-by-Chunk Breakdown ③ M社から発売された — relative clause: “released by Company M last year” ① レインコートは — topic: “as for the raincoat,” ② 機能性もさることながら — concessive set phrase: “not only (does it have) functionality,” ④ そのかわいらしいデザインが話題となり — “…its cute design became a hot topic,” leads to the result Reconstructed Sentence 去年M社から発売されたレインコートは、機能性もさることながら、そのかわいらしいデザインが話題となり、若者の間で流行している。 → “The raincoat released last year by Company M, not only functional but also with a cute design that drew attention, has become popular among young people.” Why This Order Works Topic (レインコートは) is first set by its modifier (M社から発売された). The concessive 〜もさることながら contrasts functionality with the following appeal (デザインが話題となり), leading naturally to the trend result.
questions.expl_ko: 정답: 2번 (機能性もさることながら, 정답 위치 ★). 배열: 去年 → M社から発売された → レインコートは → ★(機能性もさることながら) → そのかわいらしいデザインが話題. 「기능성은 물론이고 디자인이 화제」.

questions.stem: 「未来のものづくりコンテスト」は、______ ______ ★ ______、今年で20回目を迎える。
opts: 子供たちに
コンサートで
ものづくりの面白さを感じてもらおうと
YUUKI社が創立50周年を機に始めた
questions.expl: Answer (★ Position) Answer 4: YUUKI社が創立50周年を機に始めた (★ is 3rd) Correct Order 1 → 3 → 4 → 2 子供たちに → ものづくりの面白さを感じてもらおうと → YUUKI社が創立50周年を機に始めた → コンサートで Chunk-by-Chunk Breakdown ① 子供たちに — “to children”; indirect object/target for 感じてもらう. ③ ものづくりの面白さを感じてもらおうと — “with the aim of having (them) feel the fun of manufacturing”; purpose phrase that naturally precedes 始めた (〜ようと＋始めた). ④ YUUKI社が創立50周年を機に始めた — “YUUKI Co. started (it) on the occasion of its 50th anniversary”; main clause explaining the origin; this chunk is ★ and must sit 3rd. ② コンサートで — “at the concert/at the event”; location marker attaching to 迎える (“to mark/celebrate (the nth time)”). Reconstructed Sentence 「未来のものづくりコンテスト」は、子供たちにものづくりの面白さを感じてもらおうと、YUUKI社が創立50周年を機に始めたコンサートで、今年で20回目を迎える。 → “The ‘Future Monozukuri Contest,’ which YUUKI Co. started on its 50th anniversary to let children experience the fun of making things, marks its 20th this year at the event.” Why This Order Works - 〜ようと pairs naturally with 始めた to express purpose: 子供たちに…感じてもらおうと → 始めた. - The origin clause (YUUKI社が…始めた) explains who started it and when; placing it 3rd matches the ★ position. - The location phrase コンサートで hooks to 迎える, indicating the venue/context where the 20th will be marked. Note “コンサートで” feels unusual for a “コンテスト” context; many would expect “コンテストで.” Here we treat コンサートで as the given location phrase modifying 迎える.
questions.expl_ko: 정답: 4번 (YUUKI社が創立50周年を機に始めた, 정답 위치 ★). 배열: 子供たちに → ものづくりの面白さを感じてもらおうと → ★(YUUKI社が創立50周年を機に始めた) → コンサートで → 今年で20回目. 「YUUKI사가 50주년을 계기로 시작한 콘테스트」.

opts: 保育園
ある保育園
そうした保育園
それ以外の保育園
questions.expl: Correct Answer Answer 3: そうした保育園 is correct. It means "such nursery schools." Question Translation In those nursery schools, I am often surprised by the two-year-olds. Compared to two-year-olds in nursery schools with children up to five years old, or two-year-olds not attending nursery school, they appear very mature. Explanation The phrase "そうした保育園" refers to the nursery schools that cater to children aged zero to two years old, as mentioned in the preceding sentences. The use of "そうした" effectively links the specific type of nursery school previously described to the current observation being made about the two-year-old children. This ensures contextual coherence in discussing how these specific nursery schools appear to impact the children differently compared to other types of nursery school settings. Why Other Options Are Incorrect - Answer 1: 保育園 means "nursery school" or "preschool." It's too general and doesn't specify which type of nursery school the speaker is comparing. - Answer 2: ある保育園 means "a (certain) nursery school." It's also too general and does not specify that it's referring back to the type of nursery school previously mentioned. - Answer 4: それ以外の保育園 means "other nursery schools." It refers to the nursery schools that are not the ones being discussed, which does not match the context as it needs to specify the type that the speaker is impressed by. Grammar/Vocabulary Note "そうした" is a demonstrative adjective phrase used in Japanese to refer back to someth
questions.expl_ko: 정답: 3번 (そうした保育園, 그런 보육원). 「0~2세 한정 보육원 → 그런 보육원에 가면 놀람」. 오답: 1. 保育園 일반. 2. ある保育園(특정). 4. それ以外(그 외). 포인트: 「そうした+N」 = 앞 문장 N을 받음.

opts: なお
ただ
ゆえに
それなのに
questions.expl: Correct Answer Answer 2: ただ is correct. It means "simply" or "just." Question Translation However, between living in an environment as "I am a small child" and spending time in an environment as "I am the biggest child," I think there will obviously be differences in behavior and awareness. Explanation The sentence is contrasting two different environments in terms of their effect on children's behavior and awareness. "ただ" fits perfectly here as it introduces a contrasting explanation and simplifies the idea without adding any particular condition or reasoning. It underscores the difference purely based on environmental perception. Why Other Options Are Incorrect - Answer 1: なお (moreover, still) usually adds additional information. It does not fit the context of drawing a comparison between two situations. - Answer 3: ゆえに (therefore, consequently) implies a causal relationship which is not applicable here, as the sentence intends to present an observation rather than a cause-effect conclusion. - Answer 4: それなのに (even though) is used for expressing a contradiction, not suitable here because the sentence isn’t discussing inconsistencies or surprises. Grammar/Vocabulary Note "ただ" is often used to introduce a statement that is straightforward and uncomplicated, which works effectively here to highlight a natural comparison without delving into complex explanations. Recognizing these subtleties helps in understanding different conjunctions' uses.
questions.expl_ko: 정답: 2번 (ただ, 단). 「좋고 나쁨은 아니지만 ~」 양보 도입. 오답: 1. なお(또한). 3. ゆえに(그러므로). 4. それなのに(그럼에도). 포인트: 「ただ、~と思う」 = 「단, ~라 생각」 부분 양보.

opts: 入学したとたん
入学しておきながら
入学したとしても
入学してはじめて
questions.expl: Correct Answer Answer 1: 入学したとたん is correct. It means "as soon as one entered." Question Translation The moment they entered school, first graders became small, cute beings who were being taken care of. Explanation The phrase 入学したとたん emphasizes that the change in the role of the children—going from being the oldest and helping others to being helped by older students—happens immediately upon entering school. The context of the text discusses how quickly a child's status transitions from being independent and capable in a smaller setting to being cared for in a larger new environment like school. Therefore, "as soon as one entered" fits best to describe the immediate change in status. Why Other Options Are Incorrect - Answer 2: 入学しておきながら translates to "even though they have entered." This does not convey the immediacy of the change in the students' roles upon entering school. - Answer 3: 入学したとしても means "even if they entered." It implies a hypothetical scenario, which does not correspond to the factual description given in the passage. - Answer 4: 入学してはじめて means "only after entering," suggesting a realization or event that occurs sometime after the entrance, which does not match the instantaneous shift described. Grammar/Vocabulary Note - とたん（に） is a grammar point used to indicate that something happens immediately after something else, emphasizing the spontaneity or suddenness of the action. It's crucial in this context to show the quick change in children's roles and percepti
questions.expl_ko: 정답: 1번 (入学したとたん, 입학하자마자). 「Vたとたん」 = 「~하자마자 곧」. 오답: 2. ~ておきながら(해놓고). 3. ~としても(~라 해도). 4. ~てはじめて(비로소). 포인트: 「~たとたん」 = 즉각적 변화.

opts: たくましく思えます
たくましいとされています
たくましかったはずです
たくましくなったものです
questions.expl: Correct Answer Answer 1: たくましく思えます is correct. It means "seems strong" or "appears resilient." Question Translation Those first-graders with discontented expressions seem so resilient. Explanation The sentence describes first-grade students who seem dissatisfied, yet from the perspective of the writer, they appear resilient or strong because their discontent is tied to an expectation of growing maturity. たくましく思えます captures this nuance of observing or perceiving resilience based on expectations for the future. Why Other Options Are Incorrect - Answer 2: たくましいとされています means "it is said to be strong." This passive construction does not fit. The context does not suggest a general societal belief or consensus about their strength; instead, it's an individual observation. - Answer 3: たくましかったはずです means "should have been strong." This implies a past assumption, which doesn't align with the present observation being described in the passage. - Answer 4: たくましくなったものです means "became strong." This suggests a completed transformation, which does not match the current perception of resilience in the immediate reaction of the children. Grammar/Vocabulary Note - たくましい (takumashii) is an adjective meaning "strong," "robust," or "resilient," often used in describing someone's character, spirit, or physical appearance. - ～思えます is a form that means "seems" or "appears," showing subjective perception or a personal opinion rather than an objective statement. This makes it suitable when expressing a
questions.expl_ko: 정답: 1번 (たくましく思えます, 늠름하게 보입니다). 「불만 얼굴이 늠름하게 보임」 글쓴이 인상. 오답: 2. ~とされている(~로 여겨지다). 3. ~たはずだ(~했을 터). 4. ~ものです(~인 법). 포인트: 「~に思える」 = 「~로 보인다」 인상.

questions.stem: 筆者が、番組づくりにおいて危険だと感じていることは何か。
opts: 視聴率を上げようとして、事実とは異なることを伝えてしまうこと
わかりやすいことだけを伝えて、視聴者に関心を持たれなくなってしまうこと
わかりやすくしようとして、事実を掘り下げて伝えられなくなってしまうこと
メッセージをシンプルにしようとして、本質がわかりにくくなってしまうこと
questions.expl: Correct Answer Answer 3: わかりやすくしようとして、事実を掘り下げて伝えられなくなってしまうこと is correct. It means "In trying to make it easy to understand, they are unable to delve deeply into the facts." Question Translation What does the author feel is dangerous about program-making? Explanation The passage discusses the potential danger in creating television programs that focus too much on simplicity and clarity, which may lead to simplifying their content to the point where the depth, complexity, and richness of facts are lost. The correct answer reflects this concern, emphasizing that making programs too easy to understand can prevent a deeper exploration and presentation of the facts. Why Other Options Are Incorrect - Answer 1: 視聴率を上げようとして、事実とは異なることを伝えてしまうこと means "Trying to raise viewership ratings leads to conveying things different from the truth." This is not the danger highlighted in the passage. The passage mentions making things simple rather than altering or misrepresenting facts. - Answer 2: わかりやすいことだけを伝えて、視聴者に関心を持たれなくなってしまうこと means "Only conveying what is easy to understand leads to viewers losing interest." While simplicity is mentioned, the passage does not discuss a loss of viewer interest. - Answer 4: メッセージをシンプルにしようとして、本質がわかりにくくなってしまうこと means "By making the message simple, the essence becomes difficult to understand." This choice is somewhat similar to the correct answer, but the passage specifically points out that the danger is losing depth and richness, not making the essence unclear
questions.expl_ko: 정답: 3번 (알기 쉽게 하려다 사실을 깊이 못 전함). 본문 「わかりやすくすることでかえって、事象や事実の、深さ、複雑さ、多面性…をそぎ落としてしまう」. 오답: 1. 사실과 다름 ≠ 본문. 2. 단순한 것만 ≠ 본문 핵심. 4. 본질 어려움 ≠ 본문 표현. 포인트: 「알기 쉬움 = 깊이 상실 위험」.

questions.stem: 筆者の考えに合うのはどれか
opts: 失敗してもいいと思わせることで、相手は自ら行動できるようになる。
失敗を経験させれば、相手は失敗について自ら考えるようになる。
失敗の経験を積ませることで、相手は失敗をしないようになる。
失敗を許容しなければ、相手は行動することを恐れてしまう。
questions.expl: Correct Answer Answer 1: 失敗してもいいと思わせることで、相手は自ら行動できるようになる。 It means "By letting someone think it's okay to fail, they can act on their own." Question Translation Which statement aligns with the author's perspective? Explanation The passage discusses the importance of granting others the "right to fail," suggesting that doing so encourages self-motivation and independence. Answer 1 directly aligns with this perspective by highlighting that allowing someone the liberty to fail fosters their ability to act independently. This core idea is consistent with the author's point that providing the "right to fail" fosters spontaneity and autonomy, rather than compliance and obligation in actions. Why Other Options Are Incorrect - Answer 2: 失敗を経験させれば、相手は失敗について自ら考えるようになる。 ("By letting someone experience failure, they will ponder failure themselves.") This option misses the point; the passage emphasizes the benefit of offering the freedom to fail rather than merely considering failure itself. - Answer 3: 失敗の経験を積ませることで、相手は失敗をしないようになる。 ("By making someone accumulate failure experiences, they will avoid failing in the future.") While experience can lead to improvement, this does not align with the author's argument about fostering independence through the acceptance of failure, not prevention. - Answer 4: 失敗を許容しなければ、相手は行動することを恐れてしまう。 ("If you do not tolerate failure, the other party will become afraid of acting.") This statement, although related to fear and inaction, presents the scenario ne
questions.expl_ko: 정답: 1번 (실패해도 된다고 생각하게 함으로써 자발성). 본문 「『失敗する権利』を与えることが、相手の自発性を生み出すことに結びつく」. 오답: 2. 경험 = 부분적. 3. 실패 안 함 ≠ 본문. 4. 안 허용 시 두려움 = 부분 정답이지만 직접 표현 X. 포인트: 「실패권 = 자발성」.

questions.stem: 筆者の考えに合うのはどれか
opts: 直感力による「発見や気づき」は、問題の解決に欠かせない。
「発見や気づき」は直感力によって生まれるわけではない。
強い探求心と問題意識を持っていれば、問題は解決できる。
問題への探求心持ち続けていなければ、直感力は作用しない。
questions.expl: Correct Answer Answer 4: 問題への探求心持ち続けていなければ、直感力は作用しない。 It means "If you do not continuously have an inquisitive mind towards a problem, intuition does not work." Question Translation Which one aligns with the author's thinking? Passage: Intuition sometimes acts immediately, while at other times it may unexpectedly act after some time. In other words, it can be considered capricious. However, discoveries and realizations brought about by intuition, which may seem capricious, are actually the result of thinking continuously, worrying continuously, and strong directionality (in the note, it means consciousness geared towards a specific direction). These flashes of insight do not occur where there is no awareness or interest in inquiry. In other words, intuition works as a result of continuously having a strong inquisitive mind and problem awareness. Explanation Answer 4 is correct because the passage explains that intuition operates as a result of maintaining a strong inquisitive mind and awareness of problems. The passage emphasizes the necessity of ongoing interest and attention in inquiries for intuition to function effectively. Thus, it logically follows that without maintaining this inquisitive mindset towards the problem, intuition would not work. Why Other Options Are Incorrect - Answer 1: 直感力による「発見や気づき」は、問題の解決に欠かせない。 This statement suggests that discoveries and realizations from intuition are indispensable for solving problems, which is not explicitly stated in the passag
questions.expl_ko: 정답: 4번 (탐구심 안 가지면 직감 X). 본문 「強い探求心と問題意識を持ち続けた結果として、あとから直感が働いている」. 오답: 1. 직감 필수 ≠ 본문 핵심. 2. 직감 X 발생 ≠ 본문(직감은 결과). 3. 탐구심 = 해결 ≠ 본문(직감 발동 조건). 포인트: 「持続 탐구심 = 직감 작동」.

questions.stem: 「思い出」について、筆者の考えに合うのはどれか。
opts: 不愉快な「思い出」ほど、記憶に残りやすい。
時間がたてば、多くの「思い出」が消えていく。
時間がたつほど、事実とは異なっていくことが多い。
出来事の記憶が鮮明なほど、自分の「物語」が作られる。
questions.expl: Correct Answer Answer 3: 時間がたつほど、事実とは異なっていくことが多い。 It means, "As time passes, it is often the case that memories differ from the actual facts." Question Translation Which of the following aligns with the author’s view on "memories"? Explanation The passage describes how "思い出" (memories) consists of the emotional movements within oneself regarding past events. It indicates that unpleasant memories tend to be sealed away while pleasant ones may become exaggerated, leading to narratives that exceed the factual events. It further explains that as time passes, one tends to create memories filled with misunderstandings or false impressions, suggesting that the memory deviates from the actual facts. Hence, Answer 3 aligns well with the passage's explanation of memories becoming less factual over time. Why Other Options Are Incorrect - Answer 1: 不愉快な「思い出」ほど、記憶に残りやすい。 This means unpleasant memories are more likely to remain, but the passage suggests they are sealed away, not that they remain strong in memory. - Answer 2: 時間がたてば、多くの「思い出」が消えていく。 This means many memories fade over time. However, the passage discusses transformation into narratives rather than fading away. - Answer 4: 出来事の記憶が鮮明なほど、自分の「物語」が作られる。 This suggests that more vivid memories create personal narratives, whereas the passage indicates that time and misunderstandings, not vivid recollection, lead to narrative creation. Grammar/Vocabulary Note - 「思い出」(おもいで) means "memories." - 封印 (ふういん) indicates something that is seale
questions.expl_ko: 정답: 3번 (시간 지날수록 사실과 달라지는 경우 多). 본문 「『かつて』から経た時間が遠くなれば、思い違いや思い込みを含んだ『私の記憶』が作られる」. 오답: 1. 불쾌 기억 ≠ 본문(불쾌는 봉인). 2. 사라짐 ≠ 본문(왜곡). 4. 선명 = 物語 ≠ 본문 핵심. 포인트: 「시간 = 기억 왜곡」.

questions.stem: 料理本について筆者が不満に思っていたことは何か
opts: なぜ自分とは違う手順なのかがわからないこと。
作り方が丁寧に書かれていないこと。
手順が決められた理由が書かれていないこと。
手順が一つしか書かれていないこと。
questions.expl: Correct Answer Answer 3: 手順が決められた理由が書かれていないこと is correct. It means "The reasons why the procedures are set are not written." Question Translation What is the author dissatisfied about regarding cookbooks? Explanation The author's complaint in the passage is that cookbooks often list cooking steps like "heat the pan," "add two tablespoons of oil," "add whisked eggs," etc., but they do not explain the reasons for following these steps. The author wants to understand the fundamental principles behind each step rather than merely following a set manual, which is why answer 3 is the best fit. The author emphasizes the importance of knowing why certain procedures are followed so that the knowledge can be applied more broadly. Why Other Options Are Incorrect - Answer 1: なぜ自分とは違う手順なのかがわからないこと. This means "Not understanding why the procedures are different from oneself." The author's concern is not about the procedures being different from their own approach but about the lack of explanation for the steps. - Answer 2: 作り方が丁寧に書かれていないこと. This translates to "The method is not written in detail." The problem noted by the author is not about the detail level but the lack of explanation for performing each step. - Answer 4: 手順が一つしか書かれていないこと. This means "Only one procedure is written." The author's issue is not the number of procedures shared but the absence of reasons behind them. Grammar/Vocabulary Note The term 基本原理 (きほんげんり) means "fundamental principles." The author places value on under
questions.expl_ko: 정답: 3번 (수순이 정해진 이유가 안 적힘). 본문 「なぜそうするのかが書かれていない」. 오답: 1. 이유 모름 ≠ 정확. 2. 정성 부족 ≠ 본문. 4. 한 가지 수순 ≠ 본문 핵심. 포인트: 「수순 + 이유」가 핵심 욕구.

questions.stem: 型について、筆者はどのように述べているか
opts: 個人が自由に作ったもので、基本原理をもとに作られたものではない。
学ぶことはできないので、自分で見つけるしかない。
作った人には必要なものだが、誰にでも役に立つとは言えない。
作った人から学べば、誰にでも応用できるものだ。
questions.expl: Correct Answer Answer 3: 作った人には必要なものだが、誰にでも役に立つとは言えない. It means "It is something that was necessary for the person who created it, but it cannot be said to be useful for everyone." Question Translation Regarding "型" (kata, meaning "pattern" or "model"), what does the author say about it? Explanation Answer 3 is correct because the passage emphasizes that a "型" (model or pattern) was necessary for the person who originally created it but may not be universally beneficial. The author argues that a standardized approach, like cooking recipes, may not be applicable to everyone's needs and suggests that understanding the fundamental principles behind actions is more valuable. The passage explicitly states: "型というものは、それを考案した人自身に最も必要だったものであって、必ずしもすべての人に有益とは限らない," which aligns directly with this answer choice. Why Other Options Are Incorrect - Answer 1: 個人が自由に作ったもので、基本原理をもとに作られたものではない. This means "It is something freely created by individuals, not based on fundamental principles." This is incorrect because the passage suggests that although the "型" was necessary for its creator, it still follows some underlying principles, which the author is interested in understanding. - Answer 2: 学ぶことはできないので、自分で見つけるしかない. This means "It cannot be learned, so one must find it themselves." This is incorrect because the author believes that one should extract the essence and learn the principles to derive their own methods, rather than not being able to learn at all. - Answer 4: 作った人から学べば、誰にでも応用できるものだ.
questions.expl_ko: 정답: 3번 (만든 사람에겐 필요했지만 누구에게나 도움 X). 본문 「型は、それを考案した人自身に最も必要だったものであって、必ずしもすべての人に有益とは限らない」. 오답: 1. 자유 작성 ≠ 본문(원리 기반). 2. 못 배움 ≠ 본문. 4. 누구나 응용 ≠ 본문(반대). 포인트: 「型 = 개인적·한정적 도구」.

questions.stem: 学ぶことについて、筆者の考え合うのはどれか
opts: 基本原理を理解したうえで、自分なりのやり方を発見することが大切だ。
より多くの型を学び、その中から自分に合ったやり方を探し出すべきだ。
まず型身につけ、それを自分に合うように修正していくことが大切だ。
マニュアルを使うことをやめて、基本原理を自分で考えるべきだ。
questions.expl: Correct Answer Answer 1: 基本原理を理解したうえで、自分なりのやり方を発見することが大切だ。 It means that it is important to discover your own way after understanding the fundamental principles. Question Translation Which of the following best reflects the author's thoughts on learning? Explanation The passage discusses the author's dissatisfaction with the way cookbooks often provide instructions without explaining the reasons behind them. The author argues that understanding the basic principles allows one to apply them broadly, rather than mechanically copying individual methods. Thus, true learning involves extracting the essence from existing methods to create one's own approach. This aligns with answer 1, which emphasizes understanding fundamental principles and then discovering one's own way. Why Other Options Are Incorrect - Answer 2: より多くの型を学び、その中から自分に合ったやり方を探し出すべきだ. This suggests learning as many methods as possible and then picking one that fits, which contradicts the author's view of deriving one's unique approach from basic principles, not by selecting pre-existing models. - Answer 3: まず型身につけ、それを自分に合うように修正していくことが大切だ. This implies modifying existing models to fit, while the author advocates understanding principles rather than just altering existing methods. - Answer 4: マニュアルを使うことをやめて、基本原理を自分で考えるべきだ. While close in spirit, it suggests stopping using manuals altogether, whereas the author focuses on understanding fundamental principles rather than completely disregarding manuals. Grammar/Vocabular
questions.expl_ko: 정답: 1번 (기본원리 이해 + 자기 방법 발견). 본문 「基本原理さえつかめれば…他にも広く応用が利く…自分に合ったやり方を生み出すこと」. 오답: 2. 더 많은 형 ≠ 본문 핵심. 3. 형 修正 = 부분. 4. 매뉴얼 그만 ≠ 본문(원리 추구). 포인트: 「원리 + 자기 방법」.

questions.stem: 筆者にとって、アニメーションを作る作業とはどのようなものか
opts: 自分のイメージとおりになることを求めずに、楽しみながら形にする。
自分のイメージに近づけて修正を重ねるうちに、できあがっていく。
思い描いたことと現実の形を調整し続け、あるところで妥協する。
十分納得できるまで、完成に向けて作業を積み重ねる。
questions.expl: Correct Answer Answer 3: 思い描いたことと現実の形を調整し続け、あるところで妥協する。 This means "Continuously adjusting the image you have conceived and the reality, and compromising at a certain point." Question Translation For the author, what is the process of making animation like? Explanation The passage describes the author's experience in creating animations as a process of aligning their initial imagination with the actual images created. The passage mentions the difficulty in completely matching one's imagination with reality, often leading to a compromise ("妥協") at some point. Thus, Answer 3 accurately captures this process of ongoing adjustments and eventual compromise, making it the most suitable choice. Why Other Options Are Incorrect - Answer 1: 自分のイメージとおりになることを求めずに、楽しみながら形にする。 This means creating without seeking to match one's image and enjoying the process. This is incorrect because the passage indicates the author is continuously striving to match the image, albeit finding it difficult to achieve perfectly. - Answer 2: 自分のイメージに近づけて修正を重ねるうちに、できあがっていく。 This means it gets completed as one revises to get closer to one's image. While partially true about revising, it lacks the essential aspect of compromise at a certain point, which is emphasized in the passage. - Answer 4: 十分納得できるまで、完成に向けて作業を積み重ねる。 This means continuously working towards completion until fully satisfied. This is incorrect since the passage highlights that full satisfaction is rare, and compromise is a necessary step in the p
questions.expl_ko: 정답: 3번 (이미지와 현실 형태 조정·타협). 본문 「できあがってきたイメージと自分の頭の中のイメージとの、一致とズレを近づけたり、遠ざけたり…どこかであきらめを付けて、終わりにします」. 오답: 1. 이미지대로 안 추구 + 즐기기 ≠ 본문. 2. 수정으로 일치 ≠ 본문(타협). 4. 만족까지 ≠ 본문(일정선 양보). 포인트: 「조정 + 양보 = 완성」.

questions.stem: この魔物とは何か
opts: 我慢できないくらい大変で、作品作りをやめてしまいたくなる物作り。
後悔に悩まされながら、納得できる作品を目指し続ける物作り。
自分のベストの形が分からなくなってしまうような物作り。
作品を作り終えるたびに、気力を奪われるような物作り。
questions.expl: Correct Answer Answer 2: 後悔に悩まされながら、納得できる作品を目指し続ける物作り。 It means "creating things while being troubled by regrets, continuing to aim for a satisfactory creation." Question Translation What is this "魔物" (monster)? Explanation The passage describes the emotional and psychological journey of an animator or creator dealing with both the joy and the struggles of creative work. The phrase "この魔物" (this monster) is metaphorical, symbolizing the ongoing cycle of striving for perfection, followed by self-doubt and regret, yet not being able to stop due to the deep satisfaction found in creation. The correct answer captures the essence of this cycle—despite being plagued by regret, the creator continues to pursue a satisfactory piece of work. Why Other Options Are Incorrect - Answer 1: 我慢できないくらい大変で、作品作りをやめてしまいたくなる物作り。 This means "creating things is so unbearable that you want to quit." It doesn't accurately depict the mix of regret and joy described in the passage. The passage mentions continuation, not stopping. - Answer 3: 自分のベストの形が分からなくなってしまうような物作り。 This means "creating things such that you lose sight of your best form." Although it touches on confusion in creativity, it misses the themes of continual pursuit and regret discussed in the text. - Answer 4: 作品を作り終えるたびに、気力を奪われるような物作り。 This means "creating things so that you are drained of energy upon completion." While it addresses some negative feelings, it doesn’t capture the ongoing cycle of striving and achieving described in the pass
questions.expl_ko: 정답: 2번 (후회에 시달리며 만족 작품 추구하는 물건만들기). 본문 「もう少しこうすればよかった…後悔ばかり…次こそは自分のベストを形にしようと、日々まるで『修行』のように物作りに追われていく」. 오답: 1. 그만두고 싶음 ≠ 본문 핵심(계속). 3. 베스트 모름 ≠ 본문. 4. 의욕 박탈 ≠ 본문. 포인트: 「악마 = 끝없는 추구」.

questions.stem: 創作することについて、筆者の考えに合うのはどれか。
opts: 自分の思いどおりにならなくても、人に評価された時に充実感が得られる。
自分の世界観を思う存分広げ、作品の中で表現できた時に満足できる。
満足できる作品になりそうになくても、完成させると充実感が得られる。
いつか満足する作品が作れると信じていれば、評価される作品が作れる。
questions.expl: Correct Answer Answer 2: 自分の世界観を思う存分広げ、作品の中で表現できた時に満足できる. It means "One can feel satisfied when they can fully expand and express their worldview within their work." Question Translation Which option aligns with the author's thoughts on creation? Explanation The passage explains that while creating animation is difficult and creators often feel regret after completion, there is a significant joy found in expressing oneself. The author mentions the deep joy experienced during the creative process which cannot be matched by other enjoyable activities. Even without achieving perfection, expressing one's values through creation offers fulfillment beyond external evaluation. This idea aligns with option 2, which emphasizes the satisfaction derived from fully expressing one's worldview in a work. Why Other Options Are Incorrect - Answer 1: This suggests fulfillment comes from external evaluation, but the passage emphasizes joy from the creation process itself and expressing personal values, regardless of external opinions. - Answer 3: This implies that completion itself leads to satisfaction, but the passage suggests satisfaction comes from expressing one's worldview, not just finishing the work. - Answer 4: This focuses on the hope of making a satisfying work in the future for good evaluation. The passage stresses present fulfillment found in expressing values, not future success or external validation. Grammar/Vocabulary Note - "羽を伸ばせる" (はねをのばせる) means to "spread one's wings," a
questions.expl_ko: 정답: 2번 (자기 세계관을 작품에 표현 시 만족). 본문 「言葉にはできない価値観をうまく作品として整えられた時、それは人の反応や評価を超えた、絶対的な充実感」. 오답: 1. 평가 시 ≠ 본문(평가 초월). 3. 만족 X ≠ 본문(만족 작품). 4. 평가받는 작품 ≠ 본문 핵심. 포인트: 「자기 가치관 표현 = 절대 충실감」.

questions.stem: 筆者によると、デジタルを使うことで前頭葉の老化が進むのはなぜか
opts: 記憶にかかわる脳の機能を使うことが少なくなるから。
デジタルがもたらす膨大な情報が、脳を疲労させるから。
脳が情報や知識を扱うことがなくなるから。
脳が感情のコントロールや思考をしないようになるから。
questions.expl: Correct Answer Answer 1: 記憶にかかわる脳の機能を使うことが少なくなるから。 This answer is correct. It means "because the brain functions related to memory are being used less." Question Translation According to the author, why does the frontal lobe's aging accelerate when using digital devices? Explanation The passage explains that the frontal lobe, responsible for memory, motivation, and emotion control, ages fastest, and over-reliance on digital tools reduces the opportunities to store and recall information from our brain. This reliance on external devices as "external brains" minimizes the need to use the brain's memory capacity, thus potentially accelerating its aging process. Answer 1 correctly captures this explanation, stating that the decreased use of brain functions related to memory is a factor in the aging of the frontal lobe. Why Other Options Are Incorrect - Answer 2: デジタルがもたらす膨大な情報が、脳を疲労させるから ("because the overwhelming information from digital devices tires the brain"): This does not address why the frontal lobe specifically ages or relate to the less frequent use of brain functions for memory. - Answer 3: 脳が情報や知識を扱うことがなくなるから ("because the brain stops handling information and knowledge"): This is too broad and not supported by the passage. The text mentions reduced usage, not a complete cessation of handling information. - Answer 4: 脳が感情のコントロールや思考をしないようになるから ("because the brain stops controlling emotions or thinking"): The passage does not discuss a complete loss of these functions du
questions.expl_ko: 정답: 1번 (기억 관련 뇌 기능 사용 감소). 본문 「自分の頭の中に情報や知識を蓄積したり、繰り返し引き出す機会が減った…前頭葉の老化をいっそう促している」. 오답: 2. 정보 양 → 피로 ≠ 본문 표현. 3. 정보 X ≠ 본문(가능). 4. 감정 + 사고 ≠ 본문 핵심(기억). 포인트: 「기억 빈도 감소 = 노화」.

questions.stem: この考えとはどのような考えか
opts: 脳よりデジタルのほうが、情報や知識を多く蓄積できる。
デジタルに頼り過ぎると、次第に脳の機能が低下する。
デジタルは、脳と同じように思考に使えるものではない。
デジタルと脳を使い分けることで、脳は思考に特化できる。
questions.expl: Correct Answer Answer 4: デジタルと脳を使い分けることで、脳は思考に特化できる。 This means that the brain can specialize in thinking by differentiating between the use of digital tools and the brain. Question Translation What kind of idea is this? Explanation The passage focuses on the role of the frontal lobe in human cognition and the potential drawbacks of over-reliance on digital devices, described as an "external brain." It suggests that while digital technologies can store vast amounts of information (external memory), it's crucial to use one's own brain for memory and thought processing. The passage concludes that over-reliance on digital tools without engaging our own cognitive abilities might lead to faster aging of the frontal lobe but focusing the brain on thought processes rather than information storage can enhance its functionality. Why Other Options Are Incorrect - Answer 1: 脳よりデジタルのほうが、情報や知識を多く蓄積できる。 This is a factual statement but not the idea being explored in the passage, which emphasizes balancing digital and cognitive use. - Answer 2: デジタルに頼り過ぎると、次第に脳の機能が低下する。 While this highlights a concept discussed, it's not the core idea. The passage acknowledges this risk but proposes an approach to mitigate it (using digital tools for storage while dedicating brain use for thinking). - Answer 3: デジタルは、脳と同じように思考に使えるものではない。 This dismisses digital tools' role but doesn't align with the passage's discussion on collaboratively using digital tools to enhance brain efficiency. Grammar/Vocabulary Not
questions.expl_ko: 정답: 4번 (디지털과 뇌 분리로 뇌가 사고 특화). 본문 「外部脳を持てば、自分の脳を記憶装置にせず、思考の場として徹底できる」. 오답: 1. 디지털 > 뇌 ≠ 본문. 2. 의존 → 기능 저하 ≠ 본문(이건 글쓴이의 우려). 3. 사고에 못 씀 ≠ 본문(반대 — 사고에 씀). 포인트: 「外部脳 = 사고 특화 견해」.

questions.stem: 筆者が言いたいことは何か
opts: 情報や知識の記憶装置である脳を、さらに鍛えるべきだ。
連想や判断の機能を持てば、デジタルも脳の役目を果たせる。
デジタルをうまく活用しなければ、脳の潜在力が引き出せない。
脳の機能を衰えさせないよう、デジタルの利用は慎重であるべきだ。
questions.expl: Correct Answer Answer 4: 脳の機能を衰えさせないよう、デジタルの利用は慎重であるべきだ。 This means that to prevent the decline of brain functions, one should be cautious about relying on digital devices. Question Translation What does the author want to say? Explanation The passage discusses how reliance on digital devices like smartphones and computers may accelerate the aging of the frontal lobes in the brain. It suggests that while digital devices provide an "external brain" that can save information, over-reliance on them could be detrimental to our own brain's health. The passage concludes by emphasizing the importance of using our own brain for storing knowledge and making judgments to retain its potential and prevent aging. Therefore, the suggestion is to be cautious about digital reliance, which aligns with Answer 4's warning about digital usage. Why Other Options Are Incorrect - Answer 1: The statement suggests further strengthening the brain's role as a memory storage device. While part of the passage speaks to the importance of maintaining brain function, it doesn't suggest that the brain should be primarily a memory storage device but instead emphasizes holistic use. - Answer 2: This answer implies digital devices can totally take over brain roles if they can perform association and judgment functions. However, the passage warns against such reliance, arguing that it's risky. - Answer 3: This suggests without digital tools, the potential of the brain cannot be realized, but the passage suggests
questions.expl_ko: 정답: 4번 (뇌 기능 약화 안 되도록 디지털 신중 사용). 본문 「闇雲にデジタルへ脳の役目を託すことは、危険を伴う…自分の脳を鍛え、活用することの重要性を再認識」. 오답: 1. 더 단련 ≠ 본문 직접. 2. 디지털 = 뇌 역할 ≠ 본문(반대). 3. 잠재력 = 디지털 ≠ 본문. 포인트: 「디지털 신중 + 뇌 鍛錬」.

questions.stem: 筆者は先生の言葉を聞いて、なんのために読書をするようになったか
opts: 難解な物の見方や考え方を理解するため。
自身の物の見方や考え方を再考するため。
自身の知識を増やすため
自身の知識が正しいかどうか、確認するため。
questions.expl: Correct Answer Answer 1: 難解な物の見方や考え方を理解するため。 It means "To understand complex perspectives and thoughts." Question Translation What was the reason the author started reading after listening to the teacher's words? Explanation The correct answer highlights the reason the author changed their approach to reading after hearing their teacher's words. The passage indicates that the author's teacher emphasized reading as engaging with material that was not yet understood to foster intellectual growth. The author then began to read the works of French philosophers and thinkers, which were particularly challenging for them, with the intention of eventually understanding these complex and difficult ideas. Why Other Options Are Incorrect - Answer 2: 自身の物の見方や考え方を再考するため (To reconsider one's own viewpoints and thoughts) is not entirely correct. While the author reflects on their perspectives, the main point after the teacher's advice was to tackle challenging materials for greater understanding, not merely reconsider personal viewpoints. - Answer 3: 自身の知識を増やすため (To increase one's knowledge) is not specific enough. The decision was more about understanding difficult concepts rather than just broadly increasing knowledge. - Answer 4: 自身の知識が正しいかどうか、確認するため (To confirm if one's knowledge is correct) doesn’t fit. The teacher’s advice and the author’s subsequent effort were about exploring the unknown and difficult, not verifying existing knowledge. Grammar/Vocabulary Note - 難解 (なんかい): "Difficult
questions.expl_ko: 정답: 1번 (난해한 견해·생각 이해 위해). 본문 「それまでに知った限られた知識を確認するだけの本を読むのではだめで、自分の知らない考え方や感じ方にふれることによって…難解であったフランス現代思想の哲学者…理解できるようになるまでとことん読む」. 오답: 2. 자기 사고 재고 = 표현은 정확하지만 「난해」 부분이 더 정확. 3. 지식 늘림 ≠ 본문. 4. 지식 정확 ≠ 본문. 포인트: 「난해 + 자기 견해 도전」.

questions.stem: 理解するとはどういうことか。
opts: 概念や観念に頼らず、人や物を中立的に捉えること。
概念や観念にとらわれず、人や物の本質を捉えること。
概念や観念を利用して、人や物を単独な在り方で捉えること。
概念や観念をとおして、様々な人や物を共通の枠組みで捉えること。
questions.expl: Correct Answer Answer 4: 概念や観念をとおして、様々な人や物を共通の枠組みで捉えること. It means "Through concepts and notions, understanding various people and things within a common framework." Question Translation What does understanding mean? Explanation The passage discusses the philosophical idea of understanding through concepts or notions, arguing that while concepts help in understanding diverse entities uniformly, they might overlook the unique aspects of those entities. The correct answer reflects this idea by stating that understanding involves perceiving diverse people and things in a common manner through the use of concepts and notions. It captures the essence of the passage, where the role of concepts is to provide a framework for understanding. Why Other Options Are Incorrect - Answer 1: 概念や観念に頼らず... "Without relying on concepts and notions" doesn't align with the passage, which focuses on the use of concepts for understanding. - Answer 2: ...本質を捉えること "Capturing the essence" suggests focusing on uniqueness, contrary to the passage's emphasis on commonality. - Answer 3: ...単独な在り方で捉えること "Capturing in a singular manner" conflicts with the discussion about common frameworks used to understand diversity. Grammar/Vocabulary Note - 概念 (がいねん) means "concept," and 観念 (かんねん) means "notion" or "idea." Both are used to generalize and understand various entities. - 理にかなった (りにかなった) means "rational" or "reasonable," highlighting the logical aspect of understanding versus a more subjective perception. - T
questions.expl_ko: 정답: 4번 (개념·관념 통해 사람·물건을 공통 틀로). 본문 「唯一存在しているこの人やこの物を、その単独な在り方ではなく、ほかの人やほかの物と共通する一般的な在り方で捉える」. 오답: 1. 중립적 = 본문 표현이지만 「개념 X」 X. 2. 본질 ≠ 본문 핵심. 3. 단독 ≠ 본문(공통). 포인트: 「이해 = 일반화·공통화」.

questions.stem: 理解することの問題点について、筆者はどのように考えているか
opts: 学問としての正しい在り方に、疑問を持たなくなる。
諸々の存在を個別性や独自性でしか捉えられなくなる。
人や物の個別性や独自性が見えなくなるおそれがある。
人や物の本質に力を加えて、ゆがめてしまう可能性がある。
questions.expl: Correct Answer Answer 3: "人や物の個別性や独自性が見えなくなるおそれがある" is correct. It means "There is a risk that the individuality and uniqueness of people or things will become difficult to see." Question Translation What does the author think about the problems of understanding? Explanation The passage discusses the complexity and potential limitations of understanding as it deeply examines how understanding is achieved through concepts and ideas that generalize subjects. The author points out that while these concepts are correct and useful for understanding, they may overlook the distinctive, singular aspects of people and things. Specifically, the passage highlights a concern that when we seek to understand something, the unique characteristics of an individual or an object might remain unnoticed, as understanding tends to focus on commonalities rather than on what makes something uniquely itself. Answer 3 directly addresses this issue by stating that there is a danger that individuality and uniqueness might become less visible. Why Other Options Are Incorrect - Answer 1: Suggests that one becomes unquestioning of the academic approach, but the passage focuses on the limitations of understanding rather than an unquestioned adherence to scholarly methods. - Answer 2: States that things can only be perceived individually or uniquely, which contradicts the passage's theme that understanding favors generalization over recognizing unique characteristics. - Answer 4: Posits that the essence of
questions.expl_ko: 정답: 3번 (사람·물건의 개별성·독자성을 못 보게 됨). 본문 「単独のなにかは、理解では捉えられないまま、知それ自体のなかで忘却されてしまいがち」. 오답: 1. 학문 의문 X ≠ 본문. 2. 개별성으로만 ≠ 본문(반대). 4. 본질 변형 ≠ 본문. 포인트: 「이해 = 단독성 망각」.

questions.stem: AとBの認識で共通していることは何か
opts: 初対面の印象を変えるのは難しい。
態度や服装も相手が持つ印象に影響を与える。
相手が持つ印象を気にしすぎないほうがいい。
信頼度は、自分自身の努力だけでは高められない。
questions.expl: Correct Answer Answer 2: 態度や服装も相手が持つ印象に影響を与える is correct. It means "Attitude and appearance also influence the impression one gives to others." Question Translation What is common in the recognition of A and B? Explanation Both passages emphasize the importance of making a good impression through one's appearance and manner. In A, it is highlighted that the minimal manners such as style of speech and attire are essential, as a poor first impression can be difficult to overcome. In B, the passage stresses the importance of giving a trustworthy impression as a first step to enhancing persuasiveness, mentioning how business people care about their clothing and language to present a reliable image. Therefore, the shared concept between A and B is that one's attitude and appearance impact how others perceive them, making answer 2 the best fit. Why Other Options Are Incorrect - Answer 1: 初対面の印象を変えるのは難しい ("It is difficult to change the first impression.") is mentioned in A, but not in B, which focuses on building trust and expertise. Thus, it is not a shared point. - Answer 3: 相手が持つ印象を気にしすぎないほうがいい ("It's better not to worry too much about the impression one gives others.") is discussed in A, but B emphasizes creating a trustworthy impression, which doesn't suggest disregarding how one is perceived. - Answer 4: 信頼度は、自分自身の努力だけでは高められない ("Trustworthiness cannot be improved solely by one's own efforts.") is not supported by either passage. Both passages discuss individual efforts in enha
questions.expl_ko: 정답: 2번 (태도·복장도 인상에 영향). A: 「最低限のマナーは身につけておかなければならない」, B: 「服装や言葉づかいを気にする」. 오답: 1. 첫인상 변경 어려움 ≠ 양쪽. 3. 인상 신경 X ≠ A 부분. 4. 노력 X ≠ B 표현. 포인트: 「외양 = 인상 영향」.

A does not emphasize this point. - Answer 2: This option incorrectly suggests that both A and B focus on understanding the audience's requirements by actively listening, which is not a central theme in either A or B's passages. - Answer 4: Implies that A focuses on accurately explaining what the audience seeks and B talks about adjusting the quantity of information. While A does address tailored explanations, B's focus is on gathering and effectively using spe
questions.expl_ko: 정답: 3번 (A는 상대 맞춤 설명, B는 전문지식+생활 결합). A: 「相手の性格や価値観、知的水準などに応じた説明のしかた」, B: 「専門的知識や情報を生活に密着した形で提示」. 오답: 1. 일상 예 ≠ A. 2. 듣기 ≠ A 핵심. 4. 정보량 조절 ≠ B. 포인트: A=상대 적응, B=전문성+생활 결합.

questions.stem: 筆者によると、自然界で子孫を残した生物はなぜ子孫を残せたか
opts: 自然環境に合わせて自身を変化させたから。
自身に適した自然環境を選んで移動したから。
DNAが複製されるときに、ずれが生じなかったから。
DNAに生じた変化が、たまたま自然環境に合ったから。
questions.expl: Correct Answer Answer 4 is correct. It means "The changes that occurred in the DNA happened to match the natural environment." Question Translation According to the author, why were organisms able to leave descendants in the natural world? Explanation The passage explains that genetic changes (mutations) occur randomly in DNA during replication. These mutations themselves have no inherent purpose or direction. The passage states that the natural environment selects suitable changes, allowing those organisms to survive and reproduce. In this context, the answer implies that the DNA changes were coincidentally beneficial, aiding the organism's survival and their ability to reproduce and leave descendants. Thus, Answer 4 correctly reflects this: the changes that randomly occurred in the DNA happened to be advantageous in the current natural environment. Why Other Options Are Incorrect - Answer 1: 自然環境に合わせて自身を変化させたから (Because they adapted themselves to the natural environment) - This suggests intentional adaptation by the organisms, which contradicts the passage's emphasis that changes occur by chance, not through conscious effort. - Answer 2: 自身に適した自然環境を選んで移動したから (Because they chose a suitable natural environment and moved to it) - This implies organisms intentionally relocated to favorable environments, which is not mentioned or supported by the text. - Answer 3: DNAが複製されるときに、ずれが生じなかったから (Because discrepancies did not occur when DNA was replicated) - This option contradicts the
questions.expl_ko: 정답: 4번 (DNA 변화가 우연히 환경에 맞아서). 본문 「自然環境に適した変化は選択され、子孫を残す」. 오답: 1. 자기 변화 ≠ 본문(우연). 2. 환경 선택 이동 ≠ 본문. 3. 어긋남 X ≠ 본문(어긋남이 변화). 포인트: 「변화 = 우연, 선택 = 환경」.

questions.stem: ①ホワイトレグホンについて、筆者はどのように述べているか
opts: 卵を産んでも暖めない。
一度に多くの卵を産む。
産んだ卵をほかの鳥に託す。
ほかの鳥より卵を暖める時間が短い。
questions.expl: Correct Answer Answer 1: 卵を産んでも暖めない。 It means "They lay eggs but do not warm them." Question Translation How does the author describe the White Leghorn? Explanation The passage explains that White Leghorns are chickens that have retained a mutation allowing them to lay eggs without necessarily incubating them. Humans have taken on the role of incubating these eggs themselves. This allows the White Leghorns to lay a large number of eggs, over 300 per year, because they don’t spend time brooding. Why Other Options Are Incorrect - Answer 2: 一度に多くの卵を産む (They lay many eggs at once). The passage describes the White Leghorn laying eggs daily, rather than laying many at once. Therefore, this option does not align with the information given. - Answer 3: 産んだ卵をほかの鳥に託す (They entrust their eggs to other birds). This is not mentioned. The passage states that humans intervene, not other birds. - Answer 4: ほかの鳥より卵を暖める時間が短い (They spend less time warming eggs than other birds). The passage indicates they do not warm their eggs at all, making this option inaccurate. Grammar/Vocabulary Note - 卵 (たまご) means "egg". - 暖める (あたためる) translates to "to warm" or "to incubate" in this context. - The phrase "選びとった" suggests an intentional selection by humans, a crucial aspect to understanding how human intervention alters natural selection in the passage.
questions.expl_ko: 정답: 1번 (알을 낳아도 안 따뜻하게). 본문 「卵を産んでも暖めることを放棄してしまった…毎日のように卵を産み続けるニワトリ」. 오답: 2. 한 번에 多 ≠ 본문(매일). 3. 다른 새에 ≠ 본문. 4. 짧은 시간 ≠ 본문. 포인트: 「保温 X + 다산」.

questions.stem: カイコについて、筆者はどのように述べているか。
opts: 身動きが取れないため、絹糸が多く作り出せなくなった。
人間が餌を与えることで、絹糸を作ることに専念するようになった。
生きるエネルギーの大部分を、餌を食べることに費やすようになった。
人工的な環境を与えた結果、絹糸を作るという新しい能力を獲得した。
questions.expl: Correct Answer Answer 2: 人間が餌を与えることで、絹糸を作ることに専念するようになった。 This means "By humans providing food, it became able to focus on silk production." Question Translation What does the author say about the silkworm? Explanation The passage describes how humans have intervened in the natural evolution of silkworms (カイコ), providing them with a constant food supply and an artificial environment. This intervention allowed silkworms to no longer need to search for food on their own, and instead, redirect their energy toward silk production. Therefore, they were selectively bred by humans for this purpose. Thus, Answer 2 accurately reflects the content of the passage where the silkworms focus on silk production due to the provision of food by humans. Why Other Options Are Incorrect - Answer 1: "身動きが取れないため、絹糸が多く作り出せなくなった。" (Unable to move, they cannot produce much silk.) This is incorrect because the passage states that silkworms were made to produce more silk and that being less mobile allowed them to produce larger cocoons, not less. - Answer 3: "生きるエネルギーの大部分を、餌を食べることに費やすようになった。" (They began to spend most of their life energy eating.) Incorrect because the passage emphasizes reduced energy spent on finding food, shifting focus to producing silk instead. - Answer 4: "人工的な環境を与えた結果、絹糸を作るという新しい能力を獲得した。" (As a result of being given an artificial environment, they acquired a new ability to make silk.) While the environment helped, the passage indicates that silk production is not a new ability bu
questions.expl_ko: 정답: 2번 (인간이 먹이 주어 누에가 견사 만들기에 전념). 본문 「人間が常時、餌を供給して人工的な環境を与えた…絹糸を作り出す能力に振り向けるような種が選別」. 오답: 1. 못 움직여 견사 X ≠ 본문(반대). 3. 먹이에 에너지 ≠ 본문. 4. 새 능력 획득 ≠ 본문(있던 능력 강화). 포인트: 「인간 사육 = 견사 능력 특화」.

questions.stem: 筆者が言いたいことは何か。
opts: 人間が作りかえた生物は、本来の姿に戻さなければならない。
人間はあらゆる生物と新たな共生関係を築くべきだ。
人間は生物の進化のプロセスに介入するべきではない。
人間は作り出した生命との共生関係を維持しなければならない。
questions.expl: Correct Answer Answer 4: 人間は作り出した生命との共生関係を維持しなければならない。 It means "Humans must maintain the symbiotic relationship with the life they have created." Question Translation What does the author want to convey? Explanation The passage discusses how humans have intervened in the evolutionary process, creating organisms that depend on humans for survival. It points out the responsibility humans have towards these organisms and the new symbiotic relationships that have formed. Thus, the author's main message is that humans must maintain these relationships responsibly, which aligns with Answer 4. Why Other Options Are Incorrect - Answer 1: Suggests reversing the evolution of human-altered organisms, which contradicts the passage's acceptance of the new evolutionary path. - Answer 2: Suggests establishing relationships with all organisms. While the passage discusses symbiosis, its focus is on human-altered organisms, not all organisms. - Answer 3: Argues against human intervention in evolution, but the passage acknowledges that this intervention has already occurred and focuses on managing its consequences. Grammar/Vocabulary Note The word "共生関係" (きょうせいかんけい) refers to a symbiotic relationship, which is crucial in understanding the author's highlighted theme of mutual dependency and responsibility between humans and the organisms they have affected.
questions.expl_ko: 정답: 4번 (인간이 만든 생명과의 공생 관계 유지). 본문 「人間と人間が作り出した生命との新たな共生関係…人間もまたそんな生命に助けられ、あるいは支えられて生きている」. 오답: 1. 본래 모습 X ≠ 본문. 2. 모든 생물 ≠ 본문(만든 생명). 3. 개입 X ≠ 본문(이미 개입). 포인트: 「인간 = 만든 생명에 책임」.

questions.stem: ジェンカさんは、9月に受講料9,000円のビジネスマナー講座を、10月に受講料27,000円のプレゼン講座を受講する。ジェンカさんが会社から受けられる補助は、いくらになるか。
questions.expl: Correct Answer Answer 1 is correct. It means the company will reimburse ¥9,000 for the September course and ¥10,000 for the October course. Question Translation “Ms. Jenka will take a Business Manners course (fee: ¥9,000) in September and a Presentation course (fee: ¥27,000) in October. How much support can she receive from the company?” Explanation Both course fees are under ¥30,000, so she must apply under plan B (cannot mix A and B in the same year). Under B, the subsidy is ¥10,000 per course, but if a fee is under ¥10,000, the company pays the actual cost. Therefore: - September (¥9,000) → reimburse ¥9,000 (actual cost) - October (¥27,000) → reimburse ¥10,000 Why Other Options Are Incorrect 1) 9,000円と10,000円 — This is correct. 2) 9,000円と27,000円 → “¥9,000 and ¥27,000.” The company does not pay the full ¥27,000 under B; it caps at ¥10,000 per course (unless the fee is under ¥10,000). 3) 10,000円と10,000円 → “¥10,000 and ¥10,000.” The first course costs ¥9,000, and when the fee is under ¥10,000, the reimbursement is the actual amount (¥9,000), not ¥10,000. 4) 10,000円 → “¥10,000 (only).” Under B you can claim up to two courses in the year; both listed courses are eligible, so there are two reimbursements, not one. Grammar/Vocabulary Note - 未満 = “less than”; 以上 = “greater than or equal to.” - Under plan B: ¥10,000 per course (max two), but if the fee is < ¥10,000, reimburse actual cost. Also, A and B cannot be combined in the same fiscal year.
questions.expl_ko: 정답: 1번 (9000엔 + 10000엔). 비즈니스 매너 강좌 9000엔 (1만 미만 → 실비). 프레젠 강좌 27000엔 (1만 이상 → 상한 10000엔). 오답: 2. 27000 = 상한 적용 X. 3. 10000 + 10000 = 9000 받아야 함. 4. 동일. 포인트: 「1만 미만 = 실비, 이상 = 상한 1만」.

questions.stem: 森田さんは、2月20日にビジネス文書講座を受講する。受講料補助の申請はどうしなければならないか。
opts: 2月15日までに①～④を人事課に提出する。
2月15日までに①を、3月10日までに②～④を人事課に提出する。
2月15日までに①を、3月31日までに②～④を人事課に提出する。
3月10日までに①～④を人事課に提出する。
questions.expl: Correct Answer Answer 2 is correct. Question Translation “Mr. Morita will take a Business Writing course on February 20. How must he submit the subsidy application?” Explanation The general submission deadline is February 15. Because the course is after that date (Feb 20), he cannot submit all documents by the deadline. The notice states that if you can’t submit everything by the deadline, submit ① by the deadline, and submit ②–④ by March 10. Therefore, he must file ① by Feb 15 and ②–④ by Mar 10. Why Other Options Are Incorrect 1) 2月15日までに①～④を人事課に提出する。 → “Submit ①–④ to HR by Feb 15.” Impossible, since the course is on Feb 20; documents like the receipt and attendance proof aren’t available yet. 3) 2月15日までに①を、3月31日までに②～④を人事課に提出する。 → “Submit ① by Feb 15 and ②–④ by Mar 31.” The grace period for the remaining documents is until Mar 10, not Mar 31. (Mar 31 is the payment date in this special handling.) 4) 3月10日までに①～④を人事課に提出する。 → “Submit ①–④ by Mar 10.” ① must still be submitted by Feb 15; you cannot delay ① past the deadline. Grammar/Vocabulary Note - 提出締め切り = submission deadline. - Special rule: If you can’t compile everything by the deadline, submit only ① by the deadline and ②–④ by Mar 10; the subsidy will be transferred on Mar 31.
questions.expl_ko: 정답: 2번 (2/15까지 ①, 3/10까지 ②~④). 비즈니스 문서 강좌 = 2/20 수강 → 신청 마감 2/15까지 모든 서류 제출 불가. 따라서 ①만 마감 내, 나머지는 3/10까지. 오답: 1. ①~④ 동시는 시기 불가. 3. 3/31 ≠ 규정. 4. ①도 3/10이면 마감 위반. 포인트: 「① 미리 + 나머지 3/10」 분할 제출 규정.
